ai.long: AI agent task management for core-agentic integration. Provides commands to list, claim, update, and complete tasks from the agentic task queue. Includes RAG tools and metrics.
ai.claude.long: Claude Code integration for AI-assisted development workflows.
ai.claude.run.short: Run Claude Code on current directory
ai.task.short: Show task details or auto-select a task
ai.task.long: Show details of a specific task or auto-select the next available task from the queue.
ai.task_pr.short: Create a pull request for a task
ai.task_pr.branch_error: cannot create PR from {{.Branch}} branch; create a feature branch first
ai.metrics.short: View AI and security event metrics
ai.metrics.long: View collected metrics from AI tasks, security scans, and job creation events. Reads JSONL event logs from ~/.core/ai/metrics/.
ai.metrics.none_found: No events recorded in this period.
ai.label.title: Title:
ai.task_commit.long: Automatically commit staged changes with a message referencing the current task ID and title.
ai.task_complete.failed: Failed to mark task as completed.
ai.task_complete.flag.failed: Mark task as failed instead of completed
ai.task_complete.long: Mark a claimed task as completed or failed. Updates the task status in the agentic queue.
ai.task_pr.flag.base: Base branch for the pull request
ai.task_pr.flag.title: Pull request title override
ai.task_pr.long: Create a pull request for the current task. Auto-generates title and description from the task context.
ai.task_update.flag.notes: Notes to add to the task
ai.task_update.flag_required: At least one of --status, --progress, or --notes is required.
ai.task_update.long: Update the status, progress, or notes on a claimed task in the agentic queue.
ai.tasks.flag.limit: Maximum number of tasks to show
ai.tasks.flag.priority: Filter by priority (critical, high, medium, low)
ai.tasks.hint: Use 'core ai task <id>' to view details or 'core ai task --auto' to claim the next one.
ai.tasks.long: List available tasks from the core-agentic task queue. Supports filtering by status, priority, labels, and project.
ai.tasks.none_found: No tasks found matching the criteria.

build.short: Build projects with auto-detection and cross-compilation
build.long: Build projects with automatic project type detection and cross-compilation support. Supports Go, Wails, Node.js, and PHP projects.
build.flag.image: Docker image name for container builds
build.flag.type: Project type override (go, wails, node, php)
build.error.invalid_target: invalid target format "{{.Target}}", expected OS/arch (e.g., linux/amd64)
build.error.no_project_type:
no supported project type detected in {{.Dir}}
Supported types: go (go.mod), wails (wails.json), node (package.json), php (composer.json)
build.pwa.short: Build from a live PWA URL
build.pwa.flag.url: URL of the PWA to build
build.pwa.error.no_manifest_tag: no manifest link tag found in HTML
build.sdk.short: Generate API SDKs from OpenAPI spec
build.sdk.long: Generate API SDKs from an OpenAPI specification file. Supports multiple languages including TypeScript, Go, and PHP.
build.sdk.flag.dry_run: Show what would be generated without writing files
build.sdk.label: SDK
build.sdk.dry_run_mode: (dry run - no files will be written)
build.release.short: Build, archive, and publish a release
build.release.long: Build all targets, create archives, generate checksums, and publish to configured destinations. Requires .core/release.yaml configuration.
build.release.flag.go_for_launch: Actually publish to configured targets (default: dry-run only)

ci.long: Publish releases to GitHub with automatic changelog generation. Runs in dry-run mode by default for safety.
ci.init.long: Initialize release configuration for the current project. Creates a default release config file.
ci.changelog.long: Generate a changelog from git history between two refs. Uses conventional commit messages to categorise changes.
ci.changelog.flag.to: Ending ref (tag or commit SHA, default: HEAD)
ci.version.long: Show the current project version or set a new one. Reads from and writes to the version file.
ci.init.edit_config: Edit .core/release.yaml to configure your release pipeline.
ci.init.run_ci: Run 'core ci' to publish a release.

collect.long: Data collection tools for gathering information from forums, GitHub, academic papers, and market sources. Process and organise collected data.
collect.flag.dry_run: Show what would be collected without writing files
collect.bitcointalk.long: Scrape and archive a BitcoinTalk topic thread by ID or URL. Saves posts with metadata.
collect.dispatch.long: Dispatch and manage data collection events via webhook hooks.
collect.excavate.long: Excavate a project's full history across forums, repos, and archives. Discovers related resources and builds a timeline.
collect.github.long: Collect issues, pull requests, and metadata from a GitHub repository or organisation.
collect.github.flag.org: Collect all repos in the organisation
collect.market.long: Collect market data for a cryptocurrency including price, volume, and market cap from aggregator APIs.
collect.papers.long: Search for and collect academic papers from arxiv, IACR, and other sources. Downloads PDFs and extracts metadata.
collect.process.long: Process previously collected raw data from a source directory. Normalises, deduplicates, and generates summaries.

deploy.long: Infrastructure deployment tools for managing Coolify servers, projects, applications, databases, and services.

dev.long: Multi-repo development workflow tools for managing federated monorepos. Provides health checks, commit assistance, push/pull operations, and CI status across all repositories.
dev.workflow.long: Manage GitHub Actions workflows across repositories. List, sync, and update workflow files.
dev.workflow.list.long: List GitHub Actions workflow files across all repositories in the workspace.
dev.workflow.sync.long: Sync a GitHub Actions workflow file to all repositories that match the pattern.
dev.workflow.sync.flag.dry_run: Show what would be synced without making changes
dev.health.short: Quick health check across all repos
dev.health.long: Shows a summary of repository health across all repos in the workspace.
dev.push.long: Push commits to remote across all repos in the workspace.
dev.push.all_up_to_date: All repos are up to date.
dev.push.diverged_help: Some repos have diverged (local and remote have different commits).
dev.push.uncommitted_changes_commit: You have uncommitted changes. Commit with Claude first?
dev.work.use_commit_flag: Use --commit to have Claude create commits
dev.issues.short: List open issues across all repos
dev.reviews.short: List PRs needing review across all repos
dev.ci.short: Check CI status across all repos
dev.impact.short: Show impact of changing a repo
dev.impact.requires_registry: impact analysis requires repos.yaml with dependency information
dev.sync.short: Synchronizes public service APIs with internal implementations
dev.vm.not_installed: dev environment not installed (run 'core dev install' first)
dev.file_sync.long: Safely sync files or directories across multiple repositories with automatic pull/commit/push. Designed for AI agents to avoid common git pitfalls.
dev.file_sync.flag.dry_run: Show what would be done without making changes
dev.file_sync.error.no_targets: No target repos matched the pattern
dev.apply.short: Run command or script across repos (agent-safe)
dev.apply.long: Run a command or script across multiple repositories with optional commit and push. Designed for AI agents to safely apply changes at scale.
dev.apply.flag.command: Shell command to run in each repo
dev.apply.flag.script: Script file to run in each repo
dev.apply.flag.repos: Comma-separated list of repo names (default: all)
dev.apply.flag.dry_run: Show what would be done without making changes
dev.apply.flag.continue: Continue on error instead of stopping
dev.apply.warning: This will modify files in the target repos.
dev.ci.long: Check CI/CD pipeline status across all repos in the workspace. Shows pass/fail state for the latest run.
dev.commit.long: Create Claude-assisted commits across all repos with uncommitted changes. Generates descriptive commit messages.
dev.impact.long: Show which repos are affected by changes to a given repo. Uses dependency information from repos.yaml.
dev.issues.long: List open issues across all repos in the workspace. Shows issue number, title, labels, and assignee.
dev.pull.all_up_to_date: All repos are up to date.
dev.pull.flag.all: Pull all repos including clean ones
dev.pull.long: Pull the latest changes from remote across all repos in the workspace.
dev.reviews.flag.all: Show all PRs, not just yours
dev.reviews.flag.author: Filter by PR author
dev.reviews.long: List pull requests needing review across all repos in the workspace.
dev.sync.long: Synchronise public service APIs with their internal implementations. Copies interface definitions to keep packages in sync.
dev.vm.boot.long: Boot the development VM. Creates and starts the container if not already running.
dev.vm.claude.long: Run Claude Code inside the development VM with the current project mounted.
dev.vm.install.long: Install the development VM image. Downloads and sets up the container environment.
dev.vm.serve.long: Start development services inside the VM (web server, database, queue worker, etc.).
dev.vm.shell.flag.console: Open a Tinker console instead of shell
dev.vm.shell.long: Open an interactive shell session in the development VM.
dev.vm.status.long: Show the status of the development VM including resource usage and connectivity.
dev.vm.stop.long: Stop the running development VM container.
dev.vm.test.flag.name: Test name pattern to match
dev.vm.test.long: Run the project test suite inside the development VM.
dev.vm.update.long: Check for and apply updates to the development VM image.
dev.work.all_up_to_date: All repos are up to date.
dev.work.long: Multi-repo git operations. Shows status across all repos and optionally commits with Claude assistance.

docs.long: Documentation management tools for listing and syncing documentation across repositories.
docs.list.long: List documentation files across all repositories in the workspace registry.
docs.sync.long: Sync documentation files from each package into the core-php docs directory. Copies README and doc files into a unified documentation tree.
docs.sync.flag.dry_run: Show what would be synced without copying files
docs.sync.dry_run_notice: (dry run) no files will be written

doctor.long: Check development environment for required tools and configuration. Verifies git, gh CLI, language runtimes, and SSH setup.
doctor.no_repos_yaml: No repos.yaml found (run from workspace directory)
doctor.install_macos: brew install git gh php composer node pnpm docker
doctor.ssh_missing: SSH key missing - run: ssh-keygen && gh ssh-key add
doctor.check.claude.description: Claude Code CLI for AI-assisted development
doctor.check.claude.name: Claude Code
doctor.check.composer.name: Composer
doctor.check.docker.name: Docker
doctor.check.gh.name: GitHub CLI
doctor.check.git.name: Git
doctor.check.node.name: Node.js
doctor.check.php.name: PHP
doctor.check.pnpm.name: pnpm

git.long: Git workflow commands for managing repositories. Includes status, commit, push, pull operations and safe multi-repo commands for AI agents.

go.long: Go development tools including testing, formatting, linting, and module management.
go.fmt.no_changes: No changed Go files to format.
go.fmt.flag.all: Check all files, not just changed ones
go.lint.no_changes: No changed Go files to lint.
go.lint.flag.all: Lint all files, not just changed ones

php.long: Laravel and PHP development tools including testing, formatting, static analysis, security scanning, and deployment.
php.dev.long: Start a full Laravel development environment with Vite, Horizon, Redis, and Reverb. Services can be individually disabled with flags.
php.dev.flag.domain: Custom domain for the development server
php.dev.press_ctrl_c: Press Ctrl+C to stop all services
php.test.flag.group: Run only tests in specified group
php.test.flag.junit: Output results in JUnit XML format
php.fmt.short: Format PHP code with Laravel Pint
php.security.flag.sarif: Output as SARIF for GitHub Security tab
php.security.flag.url: URL to check HTTP security headers
php.qa.flag.quick: Run quick checks only (audit, fmt, stan)
php.qa.flag.full: Run all stages including slow checks
php.build.long: Build a Docker or LinuxKit image for the PHP application. Supports custom Dockerfiles, multi-platform builds, and LinuxKit templates.
php.build.flag.name: Image name
php.deploy.long: Deploy the PHP application to Coolify
php.deploy.flag.force: Force deployment even if no changes detected
php.deploy_rollback.flag.id: Specific deployment ID to rollback to
php.deploy_status.long: Show the status of a deployment
php.serve.long: Run the PHP application in a production Docker container with configurable ports and environment.
php.serve.flag.name: Container name
php.packages.long: Manage locally-developed PHP packages. Link, unlink, list, and update packages used by the application.
php.packages.link.short: Link local packages into the application
php.packages.link.long: Symlink local PHP packages into the application's vendor directory for development.
php.packages.list.long: List all locally-linked PHP packages and their paths.
php.packages.unlink.long: Remove symlinks to local PHP packages and restore the published versions.
php.packages.update.long: Update locally-linked PHP packages to their latest versions.
php.logs.long: View application logs from running containers. Supports following logs in real-time and filtering by service.
php.shell.long: Open an interactive shell session inside a running PHP container.
php.ci.short: Run CI/CD pipeline with combined reporting
php.ci.long: Run all QA checks in optimal order and generate combined reports in JSON, markdown, or SARIF format for CI/CD integration.
php.ci.flag.sarif: Generate SARIF files for static analysis
php.ci.flag.upload_sarif: Upload SARIF to GitHub Security tab
php.ci.flag.fail_on: Severity level to fail on (critical, high, warning)
php.analyse.long: Run PHPStan static analysis on the codebase. Detects type errors, undefined methods, and other issues.
php.analyse.no_analyser: No static analyser found. Install PHPStan or Psalm.
php.audit.long: Run a security audit on Composer dependencies using the Symfony Security Advisories database.
php.error.not_laravel: Not a Laravel project (no artisan file found).
php.error.not_php: Not a PHP project (no composer.json found).
php.fmt.long: Format PHP code using Laravel Pint. Shows a diff of changes or applies them with --fix.
php.fmt.no_formatter: No formatter found. Install Laravel Pint.
php.infection.long: Run mutation testing with Infection to measure test suite quality. Introduces small changes and checks if tests catch them.
php.infection.note: Note: Mutation testing can be slow on large codebases.
php.psalm.long: Run Psalm static analysis for type checking and error detection. Supports baseline generation and auto-fixing.
php.qa.long: Run the full QA pipeline: audit, format, static analysis, and tests. Use --quick for fast checks or --full for everything.
php.rector.long: Run automated code refactoring with Rector. Preview changes or apply them with --fix.
php.security.long: Run security vulnerability scanning on the PHP project. Checks dependencies, code patterns, and HTTP headers.
php.ssl.install_linux: Install mkcert: sudo apt install mkcert
php.test.long: Run PHPUnit or Pest tests with optional coverage, parallelism, and filtering.

pkg.long: Package management for core-* repositories. Clone, list, update, and search for packages.
pkg.install.long: Clone a package repository from GitHub into the workspace packages directory.
pkg.list.long: List all packages currently installed in the workspace.
pkg.update.long: Pull the latest changes for installed packages.
pkg.outdated.long: Check which installed packages have newer versions available on the remote.
pkg.search.long: Search GitHub for packages matching a pattern. Filters by organisation and package type.
pkg.search.flag.limit: Maximum number of results to return
pkg.error.invalid_repo_format: invalid repo format: use org/repo (e.g., host-uk/core-php)
pkg.error.gh_not_authenticated: GitHub CLI not authenticated. Run: gh auth login
pkg.list.install_missing: Run 'core pkg install' to add packages.
pkg.list.title: Installed packages:
pkg.outdated.all_up_to_date: All packages are up to date.
pkg.search.gh_token_warning: Set GITHUB_TOKEN for higher API limits.

sdk.short: SDK validation and API compatibility tools
sdk.long: SDK validation and API compatibility tools. Check for breaking changes and validate OpenAPI specifications.
sdk.diff.long: Compare two OpenAPI specifications and report breaking changes. Useful for CI checks before merging API changes.
sdk.diff.flag.base: Base spec file to compare against
sdk.validate.long: Validate an OpenAPI specification file for correctness and completeness.
sdk.diff.error.base_required: Base spec file is required for comparison.
sdk.diff.label: Diff

setup.short: Bootstrap workspace or clone packages from registry
setup.long: Bootstrap a new workspace or clone packages from a repos.yaml registry. Interactive wizard for selecting packages to clone.
setup.flag.dry_run: Show what would be cloned without cloning
setup.flag.name: Package name to clone
setup.flag.only: Only clone packages of this type
setup.wizard.what_to_do: What would you like to do?
setup.github.short: Configure GitHub repos with org standards
setup.github.long: Configure GitHub repositories with organisation standards including labels, webhooks, branch protection, and security settings.
setup.github.dry_run_mode: (dry run) no changes will be made
setup.github.usage_hint: Use --repo <name> for a single repo, or --all for all repos
setup.github.all_up_to_date: All repos are up to date
setup.github.error.not_authenticated: GitHub CLI not authenticated. Run: gh auth login
setup.wizard.project_name_desc: Name for the new project directory
setup.wizard.selection_hint: Use space to select, enter to confirm.

security.long: View security alerts from Dependabot, code scanning, and secret scanning across repositories.
security.alerts.short: List all security alerts across repos
security.alerts.long: List security alerts from Dependabot, code scanning, and secret scanning. Aggregates alerts across all repos in the registry.
security.deps.long: List vulnerable dependencies detected by Dependabot with upgrade recommendations.
security.scan.long: List code scanning alerts from tools like CodeQL, Semgrep, etc.
security.scan.flag.tool: Filter by tool name (e.g., codeql, semgrep)
security.secrets.long: List secrets detected by GitHub secret scanning.
security.jobs.short: Create GitHub issues from scan results
security.jobs.long: Create GitHub issues from security scan results so contributors can claim and work on them. Supports targeting external repositories.
security.jobs.flag.dry_run: Show what would be created without creating issues
security.jobs.flag.copies: Number of duplicate issues for parallel work

qa.long: Quality assurance commands for verifying work - CI status, reviews, issues.
qa.watch.short: Watch GitHub Actions after a push
qa.watch.long: Monitor GitHub Actions workflow runs triggered by a commit, showing live progress and actionable failure details.
qa.watch.error.repo_format: Invalid repo format. Use --repo org/name or run from a git repo
qa.review.long: Show PR review status with actionable next steps. Answers: What do I need to do to get my PRs merged? What reviews am I blocking?
qa.review.flag.requested: Show only PRs where your review is requested
qa.review.error.no_repo: Not in a git repository. Use --repo to specify one
qa.health.short: Aggregate CI health across all repos
qa.health.long: Shows CI health summary across all repos with focus on problems that need attention.
qa.issues.long: Show prioritised, actionable issues across all repos. Groups by: needs response, ready to work, blocked, and needs triage.
qa.issues.flag.mine: Show only issues assigned to you
qa.docblock.short: Check docstring coverage for Go code
qa.docblock.long: Analyse Go packages for docstring coverage on exported symbols. Checks functions, types, constants, and variables.
qa.docblock.use_verbose: Run with -v to see missing docstrings

test.long: Run Go tests with optional coverage reporting, race detection, and filtering.
test.error.no_go_mod: No go.mod found in current directory.

vm.long: LinuxKit VM management for running isolated development environments. Create, run, and manage lightweight VMs.
vm.run.short: Run a LinuxKit image or template
vm.run.long: Run a LinuxKit image or pre-defined template as a lightweight VM. Supports resource allocation and SSH access.
vm.run.flag.name: Name for the VM instance
vm.run.flag.ssh_port: Host port to forward to VM SSH
vm.ps.long: List all running LinuxKit VM instances with their status and resource usage.
vm.ps.flag.all: Show all VMs including stopped ones
vm.stop.long: Stop a running LinuxKit VM by container ID.
vm.logs.long: View console output logs from a LinuxKit VM instance.
vm.exec.short: Execute a command in a VM
vm.exec.long: Execute a command inside a running LinuxKit VM.
vm.templates.long: List available LinuxKit templates that can be used with 'core vm run'.
vm.templates.show.long: Show the full configuration of a LinuxKit template.
vm.templates.vars.long: Show the configurable variables for a LinuxKit template.
vm.error.id_and_cmd_required: Container ID and command are required.
vm.error.linuxkit_not_found: LinuxKit not found. Install from https://github.com/linuxkit/linuxkit
vm.error.multiple_match: Multiple containers match '{{.Name}}'. Be more specific.
vm.hint.view_logs: View logs with: core vm logs {{.ID}}
vm.label.name: Name:
vm.run.error.image_required: Image or template name is required.
vm.templates.hint.show: Show details: core vm templates show {{.Name}}
vm.templates.hint.vars: Show variables: core vm templates vars {{.Name}}
vm.templates.title: LinuxKit Templates

monitor.long: Monitor GitHub Security Tab, Dependabot, and secret scanning for actionable findings. Aggregates results from free tier scanners (Semgrep, Trivy, Gitleaks, OSV-Scanner, Checkov, CodeQL).
monitor.flag.severity: Filter by severity (critical, high, medium, low)
monitor.flag.json: Output as JSON for piping to other tools
monitor.error.no_repos: No repositories to scan. Use --repo, --all, or run from a git repo
monitor.error.not_git_repo: Not in a git repository. Use --repo to specify one

rag.long: RAG tools for storing documentation in Qdrant vector database and querying with semantic search. Eliminates need to repeatedly remind Claude about project specifics.
rag.ingest.long: Ingest markdown files from a directory into Qdrant vector database. Chunks files, generates embeddings via Ollama, and stores for semantic search.
rag.query.long: Search for similar documents using semantic similarity. Returns relevant chunks ranked by score.
rag.collections.long: List available collections, show statistics, or delete collections from Qdrant.

flag.sarif: Output as SARIF for GitHub Security tab

error.gh_not_found: 'gh' CLI not found. Install from https://cli.github.com/
